```{r}
                                     decimal.mark = ","),
                group = Type),
            position = position_dodge(width = 1),
            size = 3) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_number(scale = 1e-9, 
                                           suffix = "B", 
                                           accuracy = 1)) +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("darkgreen", "red","blue"),
                    name = NULL) +
  labs(title = "DPD 61-90",
       x = "Island",
       y = "Total Outstanding") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

ggplotly(plot2.3, tooltip = "text") %>% 
  layout(title = list(text = paste0(
      "DPD 61-90",
      "<br><sup>in billion Rupiah</sup>")),
      legend = list(orientation = "h",
                    y = -0.3))
```
The chart illustrates **Total Outstanding (in billion Rupiah)** for **DPD 61–90** across various **islands** in Indonesia, divided into **Gross**, **Net**, and **External** categories.

- **Sumatera** shows the highest exposure, reaching **100.6B Gross**, **76.2B Net**, and **62.0B External**, indicating significant outstanding amounts.  
- **Jawa** follows with **70.9B Gross**, **53.8B Net**, and **44.5B External**, while **Sulawesi** and **Papua** also record relatively high figures.  
- **Maluku** remains the lowest, with **16.9B Gross**, **12.5B Net**, and **10.7B External**.  
- Consistent with previous trends, **Gross values** exceed **Net** and **External** across all regions, reflecting standard credit adjustments.  
- Overall, the largest DPD 61–90 exposures are concentrated in **Sumatera** and **Jawa**, similar to the DPD 31–60 pattern.


### PLOT 2.4: (bar plot)
"DPD Bucket by Amount (05. 90+ DPD) ~ Total Outstanding vs Island"
```{r}
# Dataset: Gross, Net, and External
sqllab_gross_clean
sqllab_net_clean
sqllab_external_clean
```

Lastly, for the DPD bucket by amount section, we’re going to derive the latest *outstanding amount* from the *gross, net, and external dataset*, specifically for the last bucket, *“05. 90+ DPD”*, in each island.
```{r}
# Gross: Outstanding amount for "05. 90+ DPD" bucket
DPD_bucket_by_amount05_gross <- sqllab_gross_clean %>% 
  filter(bucket == "05. 90+ DPD",
         cut_off_date == "2025-11-01") %>% 
  group_by(island) %>% 
  summarise(outstanding_amount = sum(outstanding_amount)) %>% 
  ungroup()

DPD_bucket_by_amount05_gross
```

```{r}
# Net: Outstanding amount for "05. 90+ DPD" bucket
DPD_bucket_by_amount05_net <- sqllab_net_clean %>% 
  filter(bucket == "05. 90+ DPD",
         cut_off_date == "2025-11-01") %>% 
  group_by(island) %>% 
  summarise(outstanding_amount = sum(outstanding_amount)) %>% 
  ungroup()

DPD_bucket_by_amount05_net
```

```{r}
# External: Outstanding amount for "05. 90+ DPD" bucket
DPD_bucket_by_amount05_external <- sqllab_external_clean %>% 
  filter(bucket == "05. 90+ DPD",
         cut_off_date == "2025-11-01") %>% 
  group_by(island) %>% 
  summarise(outstanding_amount = sum(outstanding_amount)) %>% 
  ungroup()

DPD_bucket_by_amount05_external
```

After obtaining the latest *Gross, Net, and External outstanding amounts*, specifically for the last bucket, *“05. 90+ DPD”* bucket in each island, we're going to *merge* and *pivot* them into one dataset for easier visualization.
```{r}
# Merging Gross, Net, and External "05. 90+ DPD" bucket outstanding amounts into one dataset
DPD_bucket_by_amount05 <- DPD_bucket_by_amount05_gross %>% 
  inner_join(DPD_bucket_by_amount05_net, by = "island") %>% 
  inner_join(DPD_bucket_by_amount05_external, by = "island") %>% 
  rename(
    Gross = outstanding_amount.x,
    Net = outstanding_amount.y,
    External = outstanding_amount
  )

DPD_bucket_by_amount05
```

```{r}
# Pivot and adding NPL type column 
DPD_bucket_by_amount05 <- DPD_bucket_by_amount05 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Gross, Net, External),
    names_to = "Type",
    values_to = "Total_OS"
  ) %>% 
  mutate(Type = factor(Type, levels = c("Gross", "Net", "External")))
  
DPD_bucket_by_amount05
```

```{r}
# tooltip
DPD_bucket_by_amount05 <- DPD_bucket_by_amount05 %>%
  mutate(
    label = glue(
      "Total Oustanding:IDR {number(Total_OS, big.mark = '.', decimal.mark = ',', accuracy = 1)}"
    )
  )

DPD_bucket_by_amount05
```

```{r}
# PLOT 2.4: DPD Bucket by Amount (05. 90+ DPD)
plot2.4 <- ggplot(DPD_bucket_by_amount05, aes(x = island, 
                    y = Total_OS,
                    text = label)) +
  
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
           aes(fill = Type)) +
  geom_text(aes(y = Total_OS + 2.5e9,
                label = format(floor(Total_OS/1e8)/10,
                                     big.mark = ".",
                                     decimal.mark = ","),
                group = Type),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
            size = 3) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_number(scale = 1e-9, 
                                           suffix = "B", 
                                           accuracy = 1)) +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("darkgreen", "red","blue"),
                    name = NULL) +
  labs(title = "DPD 90+",
       x = "Island",
       y = "Total Outstanding") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

ggplotly(plot2.4, tooltip = "text") %>% 
  layout(title = list(text = paste0(
      "DPD 90+",
      "<br><sup>in billion Rupiah</sup>")),
      legend = list(orientation = "h",
                    y = -0.3))
```
The chart presents **Total Outstanding (in billion Rupiah)** for **DPD 90+** across several **islands** in Indonesia, categorized into **Gross**, **Net**, and **External** amounts.

- **Sumatera** records the highest figures, reaching **97.9B Gross**, **35.4B Net**, and **6.8B External**.  
- **Jawa**, **Sulawesi**, and **Papua** also show substantial levels, particularly in the **Gross** category.  
- **Maluku** and **Bali & Nusa Tenggara** maintain relatively lower totals, with **Gross** values under **35B**.  
- The **Gross values** remain significantly higher than **Net** and **External** across all islands, indicating a notable gap due to risk provisions or write-offs.  
- Overall, **Sumatera** continues to dominate outstanding amounts in **DPD 90+**, consistent with earlier DPD bands.


## Section 2 (DPD Bucket by Accounts)

Now, I’m going to create a similar set of **bar plots** to visualize the **total number of accounts (NOA)** across islands for each **DPD bucket**. These visualizations provide a clearer overview of accounts distribution within the `Gross`, `Net`, and `External` datasets, making it easier to compare the concentration of accounts across islands and bucket categories.

The main goals of these plots are to:

- Compare the **total number of accounts (NOA)** across `Gross`, `Net`, and `External` datasets.  
- Identify which islands have the **highest** and **lowest number of accounts** within each DPD bucket.  
- Analyze the **distribution of accounts** across islands and bucket categories.  
- Highlight the `DPD 90+` bucket to identify islands that require more attention due to having higher numbers of **overdue accounts**.

### PLOT 2.5: (bar plot)
"DPD Bucket by accounts (02. 1-30 DPD) ~ Total NOA vs Island"
```{r}
# Dataset: Gross, Net, and External
sqllab_gross_clean
sqllab_net_clean
sqllab_external_clean
```

Now, we're going to derive the latest *NOA* (Number of accounts) from the *gross dataset*, specifically for the *“02. 1–30 DPD”* bucket in each island.
```{r}
# Gross: NOA for "02. DPD 1-30" bucket
DPD_bucket_by_accounts02_gross <- sqllab_gross_clean %>% 
  filter(bucket == "02. 1-30 DPD",
         cut_off_date == "2025-11-01") %>% 
  group_by(island) %>% 
  summarise(total_noa = sum(noa)) %>% 
  ungroup()

DPD_bucket_by_accounts02_gross
```

We're also going to derive the latest *NOA* from the *net and external dataset*, specifically for the *“02. 1–30 DPD”* bucket in each island.
```{r}
# Net: NOA for "02. DPD 1-30" bucket
DPD_bucket_by_accounts02_net <- sqllab_net_clean %>% 
  filter(bucket == "02. 1-30 DPD",
         cut_off_date == "2025-11-01") %>% 
  group_by(island) %>% 
  summarise(total_noa = sum(noa))%>% 
  ungroup()

DPD_bucket_by_accounts02_net
```

```{r}
# External: NOA for "02. DPD 1-30" bucket
DPD_bucket_by_accounts02_external <- sqllab_external_clean %>% 
  filter(bucket == "02. 1-30 DPD",
         cut_off_date == "2025-11-01") %>% 
  group_by(island) %>% 
  summarise(total_noa = sum(noa)) %>% 
  ungroup()

DPD_bucket_by_accounts02_external
```

After obtaining the latest *Gross, Net, and External NOA*, specifically for the *“02. 1–30 DPD”* bucket in each island, we then *merge* and *pivot* them into one dataset for easier visualization.
```{r}
# Merging Gross, Net, and External "02. DPD 1-30" bucket NOA into one dataset
DPD_bucket_by_accounts02 <- DPD_bucket_by_accounts02_gross %>% 
  inner_join(DPD_bucket_by_accounts02_net, by = "island") %>% 
  inner_join(DPD_bucket_by_accounts02_external, by = "island") %>% 
  rename(
    Gross = total_noa.x,
    Net = total_noa.y,
    External = total_noa
  )

DPD_bucket_by_accounts02
```

```{r}
# Pivot and adding NPL type column 
DPD_bucket_by_accounts02 <- DPD_bucket_by_accounts02 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Gross, Net, External),
    names_to = "Type",
    values_to = "Total_accounts"
  ) %>% 
  mutate(Type = factor(Type, levels = c("Gross", "Net", "External")))
  
DPD_bucket_by_accounts02
```

```{r}
# tooltip
DPD_bucket_by_accounts02 <- DPD_bucket_by_accounts02 %>%
  mutate(
    label = glue(
      "Total accounts: {number(Total_accounts, big.mark = '.', decimal.mark = ',', accuracy = 1)}"
    )
  )

DPD_bucket_by_accounts02
```

```{r}
# PLOT 2.5: DPD Bucket by accounts (02. 1-30 DPD)
plot2.5 <- ggplot(DPD_bucket_by_accounts02, aes(x = island, 
                    y = Total_accounts,
                    text = label)) +
  
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
           aes(fill = Type)) +
  geom_text(aes(y = Total_accounts + 500,
                label = format(Total_accounts,
                               big.mark = ".",
                               decimal.mark = ","),
                group = Type),
            position = position_dodge(width = 1),
            size = 2.5) +
   scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma(big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ",")) +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("darkgreen", "red","blue"),
                    name = NULL) +
  labs(title = "DPD 1-30",
       x = "Island",
       y = "Total NOA") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

ggplotly(plot2.5, tooltip = "text") %>% 
  layout(title = list(text = paste0(
      "DPD 1-30")),
      legend = list(orientation = "h",
                    y = -0.3))
```
The chart shows **Total NOA** for **DPD 1–30** across different **islands** in Indonesia, categorized into **Gross**, **Net**, and **External** values.

- **Sumatera** has the **highest** total with **32,592** accounts,  
- while **Maluku** has the **lowest** with **6,638** accounts.  
- All three categories (`Gross`, `Net`, and `External`) show **identical values** across each island, indicating no variation between NPL types at this delinquency stage.


### PLOT 2.6: (bar plot)
"DPD Bucket by accounts (03. 31-60 DPD) ~ Total NOA vs Island"
```{r}
# Dataset: Gross, Net, and External
sqllab_gross_clean
sqllab_net_clean
sqllab_external_clean
```

Now, we're going to derive the latest *NOA* (Number of accounts) from the *gross, net, and external dataset*, specifically for the *“03. 31-60 DPD”* bucket in each island.
```{r}
# Gross: NOA for "03. 31-60 DPD" bucket
DPD_bucket_by_accounts03_gross <- sqllab_gross_clean %>% 
  filter(bucket == "03. 31-60 DPD",
         cut_off_date == "2025-11-01") %>% 
  group_by(island) %>% 
  summarise(total_noa = sum(noa)) %>% 
  ungroup()

DPD_bucket_by_accounts03_gross
```

```{r}
# Net: NOA for "03. 31-60 DPD" bucket
DPD_bucket_by_accounts03_net <- sqllab_net_clean %>% 
  filter(bucket == "03. 31-60 DPD",
         cut_off_date == "2025-11-01") %>% 
  group_by(island) %>% 
  summarise(total_noa = sum(noa))%>% 
  ungroup()

DPD_bucket_by_accounts03_net
```

```{r}
# External: NOA for "03. 31-60 DPD" bucket
DPD_bucket_by_accounts03_external <- sqllab_external_clean %>% 
  filter(bucket == "03. 31-60 DPD",
         cut_off_date == "2025-11-01") %>% 
  group_by(island) %>% 
  summarise(total_noa = sum(noa)) %>% 
  ungroup()

DPD_bucket_by_accounts03_external
```

We then *merge* and *pivot* all the latest *Gross, Net, and External NOA* into one dataset for easier visualization.
```{r}
# Merging Gross, Net, and External "03. 31-60 DPD" bucket NOA into one dataset
DPD_bucket_by_accounts03 <- DPD_bucket_by_accounts03_gross %>% 
  inner_join(DPD_bucket_by_accounts03_net, by = "island") %>% 
  inner_join(DPD_bucket_by_accounts03_external, by = "island") %>% 
  rename(
    Gross = total_noa.x,
    Net = total_noa.y,
    External = total_noa
  )

DPD_bucket_by_accounts03
```


```{r}
# Pivot and adding NPL type column 
DPD_bucket_by_accounts03 <- DPD_bucket_by_accounts03 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Gross, Net, External),
    names_to = "Type",
    values_to = "Total_accounts"
  ) %>% 
  mutate(Type = factor(Type, levels = c("Gross", "Net", "External")))
  
DPD_bucket_by_accounts03
```

```{r}
# tooltip
DPD_bucket_by_accounts03 <- DPD_bucket_by_accounts03 %>%
  mutate(
    label = glue(
      "Total accounts: {number(Total_accounts, big.mark = '.', decimal.mark = ',', accuracy = 1)}"
    )
  )

DPD_bucket_by_accounts03
```

```{r}
# PLOT 2.6: DPD Bucket by accounts (03. 31-60 DPD)
plot2.6 <- ggplot(DPD_bucket_by_accounts03, aes(x = island, 
                    y = Total_accounts,
                    text = label)) +
  
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
           aes(fill = Type)) +
  geom_text(aes(y = Total_accounts + 500,
                label = format(Total_accounts,
                               big.mark = ".",
                               decimal.mark = ","),
                group = Type),
            position = position_dodge(width = 1),
            size = 2.5) +
   scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma(big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ",")) +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("darkgreen", "red","blue"),
                    name = NULL) +
  labs(title = "DPD 31-60",
       x = "Island",
       y = "Total NOA") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

ggplotly(plot2.6, tooltip = "text") %>% 
  layout(title = list(text = paste0(
      "DPD 31-60")),
      legend = list(orientation = "h",
                    y = -0.3))
```
The chart illustrates **Total NOA** for **DPD 31–60** across various **islands** in Indonesia, divided into **Gross**, **Net**, and **External** categories.

- **Sumatera** has the **highest** total at **32.491** accounts,  
- while **Maluku** has the **lowest** at **5.690** accounts.  


### PLOT 2.7: (bar plot)
"DPD Bucket by accounts (04. 61-90 DPD) ~ Total NOA vs Island"
```{r}
# Dataset: Gross, Net, and External
sqllab_gross_clean
sqllab_net_clean
sqllab_external_clean
```

Now, we're going to derive the latest *NOA* (Number of accounts) from the *gross, net, and external dataset*, specifically for the *“04. 61-90 DPD”* bucket in each island.
```{r}
# Gross: NOA for "04. 61-90 DPD" bucket
DPD_bucket_by_accounts04_gross <- sqllab_gross_clean %>% 
  filter(bucket == "04. 61-90 DPD",
         cut_off_date == "2025-11-01") %>% 
  group_by(island) %>% 
  summarise(total_noa = sum(noa)) %>% 
  ungroup()

DPD_bucket_by_accounts04_gross
```

```{r}
# Net: NOA for "04. 61-90 DPD" bucket
DPD_bucket_by_accounts04_net <- sqllab_net_clean %>% 
  filter(bucket == "04. 61-90 DPD",
         cut_off_date == "2025-11-01") %>% 
  group_by(island) %>% 
  summarise(total_noa = sum(noa))%>% 
  ungroup()

DPD_bucket_by_accounts04_net
```

```{r}
# External: NOA for "04. 61-90 DPD" bucket
DPD_bucket_by_accounts04_external <- sqllab_external_clean %>% 
  filter(bucket == "04. 61-90 DPD",
         cut_off_date == "2025-11-01") %>% 
  group_by(island) %>% 
  summarise(total_noa = sum(noa)) %>% 
  ungroup()

DPD_bucket_by_accounts04_external
```

We then *merge* and *pivot* all the latest *Gross, Net, and External NOA* into one dataset for easier visualization.
```{r}
# Merging Gross, Net, and External "04. 61-90 DPD" bucket NOA into one dataset
DPD_bucket_by_accounts04 <- DPD_bucket_by_accounts04_gross %>% 
  inner_join(DPD_bucket_by_accounts04_net, by = "island") %>% 
  inner_join(DPD_bucket_by_accounts04_external, by = "island") %>% 
  rename(
    Gross = total_noa.x,
    Net = total_noa.y,
    External = total_noa
  )

DPD_bucket_by_accounts04
```

```{r}
# Pivot and adding NPL type column 
DPD_bucket_by_accounts04 <- DPD_bucket_by_accounts04 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Gross, Net, External),
    names_to = "Type",
    values_to = "Total_accounts"
  ) %>% 
  mutate(Type = factor(Type, levels = c("Gross", "Net", "External")))
  
DPD_bucket_by_accounts04
```

```{r}
# tooltip
DPD_bucket_by_accounts04 <- DPD_bucket_by_accounts04 %>%
  mutate(
    label = glue(
      "Total accounts: {number(Total_accounts, big.mark = '.', decimal.mark = ',', accuracy = 1)}"
    )
  )

DPD_bucket_by_accounts04
```

```{r}
# PLOT 2.7: DPD Bucket by accounts (04. 61-90 DPD)
plot2.7<- ggplot(DPD_bucket_by_accounts04, aes(x = island, 
                    y = Total_accounts,
                    text = label)) +
  
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
           aes(fill = Type)) +
  geom_text(aes(y = Total_accounts + 500,
                label = format(Total_accounts,
                               big.mark = ".",
                               decimal.mark = ","),
                group = Type),
            position = position_dodge(width = 1),
            size = 2.5) +
   scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma(big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ",")) +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("darkgreen", "red","blue"),
                    name = NULL) +
  labs(title = "DPD 61-90",
       x = "Island",
       y = "Total NOA") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

ggplotly(plot2.7, tooltip = "text") %>% 
  layout(title = list(text = paste0(
      "DPD 61-90")),
      legend = list(orientation = "h",
                    y = -0.3))
```
The chart displays **Total NOA** for **DPD 61–90** across several **islands** in Indonesia, grouped into **Gross**, **Net**, and **External** categories.

- **Sumatera** has the **highest** total at **34.614** accounts,  
- while **Maluku** has the **lowest** at **5.442** accounts.  


### PLOT 2.8: (bar plot)
"DPD Bucket by accounts (05. 90+ DPD) ~ Total NOA vs Island"
```{r}
# Dataset: Gross, Net, and External
sqllab_gross_clean
sqllab_net_clean
sqllab_external_clean
```

Now, we're going to derive the latest *NOA* (Number of accounts) from the *gross, net, and external dataset*, specifically for the last bucket, *“05. 90+ DPD”* in each island.
```{r}
# Gross: NOA for "05. 90+ DPD" bucket
DPD_bucket_by_accounts05_gross <- sqllab_gross_clean %>% 
  filter(bucket == "05. 90+ DPD",
         cut_off_date == "2025-11-01") %>% 
  group_by(island) %>% 
  summarise(total_noa = sum(noa)) %>% 
  ungroup()

DPD_bucket_by_accounts05_gross
```

```{r}
# Net: NOA for "05. 90+ DPD" bucket
DPD_bucket_by_accounts05_net <- sqllab_net_clean %>% 
  filter(bucket == "05. 90+ DPD",
         cut_off_date == "2025-11-01") %>% 
  group_by(island) %>% 
  summarise(total_noa = sum(noa))%>% 
  ungroup()

DPD_bucket_by_accounts05_net
```

```{r}
# External: NOA for "05. 90+ DPD" bucket
DPD_bucket_by_accounts05_external <- sqllab_external_clean %>% 
  filter(bucket == "05. 90+ DPD",
         cut_off_date == "2025-11-01") %>% 
  group_by(island) %>% 
  summarise(total_noa = sum(noa)) %>% 
  ungroup()

DPD_bucket_by_accounts05_external
```

We then *merge* and *pivot* all the latest *Gross, Net, and External NOA* into one dataset for easier visualization.
```{r}
# Merging Gross, Net, and External "05. 90+ DPD" bucket NOA into one dataset
DPD_bucket_by_accounts05 <- DPD_bucket_by_accounts05_gross %>% 
  inner_join(DPD_bucket_by_accounts05_net, by = "island") %>% 
  inner_join(DPD_bucket_by_accounts05_external, by = "island") %>% 
  rename(
    Gross = total_noa.x,
    Net = total_noa.y,
    External = total_noa
  )

DPD_bucket_by_accounts05
```

```{r}
# Pivot and adding NPL type column 
DPD_bucket_by_accounts05 <- DPD_bucket_by_accounts05 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Gross, Net, External),
    names_to = "Type",
    values_to = "Total_accounts"
  ) %>% 
  mutate(Type = factor(Type, levels = c("Gross", "Net", "External")))
  
DPD_bucket_by_accounts05
```

```{r}
# tooltip
DPD_bucket_by_accounts05 <- DPD_bucket_by_accounts05 %>%
  mutate(
    label = glue(
      "Total accounts: {number(Total_accounts, big.mark = '.', decimal.mark = ',', accuracy = 1)}"
    )
  )

DPD_bucket_by_accounts05
```

```{r}
# PLOT 2.8: DPD Bucket by accounts (05. 90+ DPD)
plot2.8 <- ggplot(DPD_bucket_by_accounts05, aes(x = island, 
                    y = Total_accounts,
                    text = label)) +
  
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
           aes(fill = Type)) +
  geom_text(aes(y = Total_accounts + 750,
                label = format(Total_accounts,
                               big.mark = ".",
                               decimal.mark = ","),
                group = Type),
            position = position_dodge(width = 1),
            size = 2.5) +
   scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma(big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ",")) +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("darkgreen", "red","blue"),
                    name = NULL) +
  labs(title = "DPD 90+",
       x = "Island",
       y = "Total NOA") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

ggplotly(plot2.8, tooltip = "text") %>% 
  layout(title = list(text = paste0(
      "DPD 90+")),
      legend = list(orientation = "h",
                    y = -0.3))
```
The chart shows **Total NOA** for **DPD 90+** across different **islands** in Indonesia, categorized into **Gross**, **Net**, and **External** values.

- **Sumatera** has the **highest** total at **33.241** accounts,  
- while **Maluku** has the **lowest** at **7.162** accounts.  


# TAB 3 (NPL DETAIL)
## Line 3.1 (NPL Net Detail: Island, Regional, Area, Branch)

In this section, we’re going to create a tab that *displays the outstanding amount, NOA, and NPL for each DPD bucket*, based on the selected *cut off date, island, region, area, and branch* in the dashboard.

### Island
For the *island level*, we’re going to create *two datasets*: the first will display the outstanding amount, NOA, and NPL for each DPD bucket across *all islands*, while the second will include a *specific cut-off date* for more detailed analysis.
```{r}
# Dataset: Net
sqllab_net_clean
```

```{r}
# ISLAND: GENERAL ~> 1 input: Cut Off Date
island_general <- sqllab_net_clean %>% 
  filter(cut_off_date == "2025-11-01") %>% 
  group_by(island) %>% 
  summarise(
    OS_Current = sum(ifelse(bucket == "01. Current", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_Current = sum(ifelse(bucket == "01. Current", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_1_30 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "02. 1-30 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_1_30 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "02. 1-30 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_31_60 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "03. 31-60 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_31_60 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "03. 31-60 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_61_90 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "04. 61-90 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_61_90 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "04. 61-90 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_90_plus = sum(ifelse(bucket == "05. 90+ DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_90_plus = sum(ifelse(bucket == "05. 90+ DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_Total = sum(outstanding_amount),
    NOA_Total = sum(noa),
    NPL= round(((OS_DPD_90_plus/OS_Total)*100),2)) %>% 
  rename(
    Island = island
  ) %>% 
  mutate(across(
    everything(),
    ~ format(.x, 
             big.mark = ".", 
             decimal.mark = ","))
    )

island_general
```


```{r}
# ISLAND: SPECIFIC ~> 2 inputs: Cut Off Date & Island
island_table <- sqllab_net_clean %>% 
  filter(cut_off_date == "2025-11-01",
         island == "Sumatera") %>% 
  group_by(island) %>% 
  summarise(
    OS_Current = sum(ifelse(bucket == "01. Current", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_Current = sum(ifelse(bucket == "01. Current", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_1_30 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "02. 1-30 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_1_30 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "02. 1-30 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_31_60 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "03. 31-60 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_31_60 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "03. 31-60 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_61_90 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "04. 61-90 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_61_90 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "04. 61-90 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_90_plus = sum(ifelse(bucket == "05. 90+ DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_90_plus = sum(ifelse(bucket == "05. 90+ DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_Total = sum(outstanding_amount),
    NOA_Total = sum(noa),
    NPL= round(((OS_DPD_90_plus/OS_Total)*100),2)) %>% 
  rename(
    Island = island
  ) %>% 
  mutate(across(
    everything(),
    ~ format(.x, 
             big.mark = ".", 
             decimal.mark = ","))
    )

island_table
```

### Region
We’re also going to apply the same approach for the *region level* by creating *two datasets*: the first will show the outstanding amount, NOA, and NPL for each DPD bucket across *all regions*, while the second will include a *specific cut-off date* for more detailed analysis.
```{r}
# REGION: GENERAL ~> 1 input: Cut Off Date 
region_general <- sqllab_net_clean %>% 
  filter(cut_off_date == "2025-11-01") %>% 
  group_by(region) %>% 
  summarise(
    OS_Current = sum(ifelse(bucket == "01. Current", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_Current = sum(ifelse(bucket == "01. Current", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_1_30 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "02. 1-30 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_1_30 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "02. 1-30 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_31_60 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "03. 31-60 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_31_60 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "03. 31-60 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_61_90 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "04. 61-90 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_61_90 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "04. 61-90 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_90_plus = sum(ifelse(bucket == "05. 90+ DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_90_plus = sum(ifelse(bucket == "05. 90+ DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_Total = sum(outstanding_amount),
    NOA_Total = sum(noa),
    NPL= round(((OS_DPD_90_plus/OS_Total)*100),2)) %>% 
  rename(
    Region = region
  ) %>% 
  mutate(across(
    everything(),
    ~ format(.x, 
             big.mark = ".", 
             decimal.mark = ","))
    )

region_general
```

```{r}
# Cross checking the total region in the Net Dataset
unique(sqllab_net_clean$region)
```
By checking the total number of regions in the dataset, we can confirm that the *region_general* dataset is correct, as it contains *37 rows*, matching the *37 regions* found in the Net dataset.

```{r}
# REGION: SPECIFIC ~> 2 inputs: Cut Off Date & Region
region_table <- sqllab_net_clean %>%
  filter(cut_off_date == "2025-11-01",
         region == "Sumatera Utara") %>% 
  group_by(region) %>% 
  summarise(
    OS_Current = sum(ifelse(bucket == "01. Current", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_Current = sum(ifelse(bucket == "01. Current", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_1_30 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "02. 1-30 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_1_30 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "02. 1-30 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_31_60 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "03. 31-60 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_31_60 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "03. 31-60 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_61_90 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "04. 61-90 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_61_90 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "04. 61-90 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_90_plus = sum(ifelse(bucket == "05. 90+ DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_90_plus = sum(ifelse(bucket == "05. 90+ DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_Total = sum(outstanding_amount),
    NOA_Total = sum(noa),
    NPL= round(((OS_DPD_90_plus/OS_Total)*100),2)) %>% 
  rename(
    Region = region
  ) %>% 
  mutate(across(
    everything(),
    ~ format(.x, 
             big.mark = ".", 
             decimal.mark = ","))
    )

region_table
```

### Area
We will also perform the same process for the area level by creating two datasets: the first will display the *outstanding amount, NOA,* and *NPL* for each *DPD bucket* across *all areas*, while the second will include a *specific cut-off date* for more detailed analysis.
```{r}
# Area: GENERAL ~> 1 input: Cut Off Date
area_general <- sqllab_net_clean %>% 
  filter(cut_off_date  == "2025-11-01") %>% 
  group_by(area) %>% 
  summarise(
    OS_Current = sum(ifelse(bucket == "01. Current", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_Current = sum(ifelse(bucket == "01. Current", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_1_30 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "02. 1-30 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_1_30 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "02. 1-30 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_31_60 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "03. 31-60 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_31_60 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "03. 31-60 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_61_90 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "04. 61-90 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_61_90 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "04. 61-90 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_90_plus = sum(ifelse(bucket == "05. 90+ DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_90_plus = sum(ifelse(bucket == "05. 90+ DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_Total = sum(outstanding_amount),
    NOA_Total = sum(noa),
    NPL= round(((OS_DPD_90_plus/OS_Total)*100),2)) %>% 
  rename(
    Area = area
  ) %>% 
  mutate(across(
    everything(),
    ~ format(.x, 
             big.mark = ".", 
             decimal.mark = ","))
    )
  
area_general
```

```{r}
# Cross checking the total areas in the Net Dataset
unique(sqllab_net_clean$area)
```
We can confirm that the area_general dataset is correct, as it contains *148 rows*, which aligns with the *148 areas* present in the Net dataset.

Similar to the previous Region: Specific section, this code first *filters* the data based on the *cut-off date* and *region.* However, this time we *group* the filtered data by *area* and then summarize it to obtain the outstanding amount, NOA, and NPL for each DPD bucket across all areas.
```{r}
# Area: SPECIFIC ~> 2 inputs: Cut Off Date & Region
area_table <- sqllab_net_clean %>%
  filter(cut_off_date == "2025-11-01",
         region == "Sumatera Utara") %>% 
  group_by(area) %>% 
  summarise(
    OS_Current = sum(ifelse(bucket == "01. Current", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_Current = sum(ifelse(bucket == "01. Current", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_1_30 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "02. 1-30 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_1_30 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "02. 1-30 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_31_60 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "03. 31-60 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_31_60 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "03. 31-60 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_61_90 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "04. 61-90 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_61_90 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "04. 61-90 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_90_plus = sum(ifelse(bucket == "05. 90+ DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_90_plus = sum(ifelse(bucket == "05. 90+ DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_Total = sum(outstanding_amount),
    NOA_Total = sum(noa),
    NPL= round(((OS_DPD_90_plus/OS_Total)*100),2)) %>% 
  rename(
    Area = area
  ) %>% 
  mutate(across(
    everything(),
    ~ format(.x, 
             big.mark = ".", 
             decimal.mark = ","))
    )

area_table
```

### Branch
Lastly, we will perform the same process for the *branch level* by creating *two datasets*: the first will display the outstanding amount, NOA, and NPL for each DPD bucket across *all branches*, while the second will include a *specific cut-off date* for more detailed analysis.
```{r}
# Branch: GENERAL ~> 1 input: Cut Off Date
branch_general <- sqllab_net_clean %>% 
  filter(cut_off_date  == "2025-11-01") %>% 
  group_by(branch) %>% 
  summarise(
    OS_Current = sum(ifelse(bucket == "01. Current", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_Current = sum(ifelse(bucket == "01. Current", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_1_30 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "02. 1-30 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_1_30 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "02. 1-30 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_31_60 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "03. 31-60 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_31_60 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "03. 31-60 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_61_90 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "04. 61-90 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_61_90 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "04. 61-90 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_90_plus = sum(ifelse(bucket == "05. 90+ DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_90_plus = sum(ifelse(bucket == "05. 90+ DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_Total = sum(outstanding_amount),
    NOA_Total = sum(noa),
    NPL= round(((OS_DPD_90_plus/OS_Total)*100),2)) %>% 
  rename(
    Branch = branch
  ) %>% 
  mutate(across(
    everything(),
    ~ format(.x, 
             big.mark = ".", 
             decimal.mark = ","))
    )
  
branch_general
```

```{r}
# Cross checking the total region in the Net Dataset
unique(sqllab_net_clean$branch)
```
We can confirm that the *branch_general* dataset is correct, as it contains *444 rows*, which matches the *444 branches* found in the Net dataset.

The branch-specific code is slightly more complex, as we first need to *filter* the data based on three inputs: *cut-off date, region,* and *area*. We then *group* the data by *area* and *branch* to obtain the specific branches within the selected area for detailed analysis.
```{r}
# BRANCH: SPECIFIC ~> 3 inputs: Cut Off Date & Region, and Area
branch_table <- sqllab_net_clean %>%
  filter(cut_off_date == "2025-11-01",
         region == "Sumatera Utara",
         area == "Sumatera Utara Area 2") %>% 
  group_by(area, branch) %>% 
  summarise(
    OS_Current = sum(ifelse(bucket == "01. Current", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_Current = sum(ifelse(bucket == "01. Current", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_1_30 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "02. 1-30 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_1_30 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "02. 1-30 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_31_60 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "03. 31-60 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_31_60 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "03. 31-60 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_61_90 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "04. 61-90 DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_61_90 = sum(ifelse(bucket == "04. 61-90 DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_DPD_90_plus = sum(ifelse(bucket == "05. 90+ DPD", outstanding_amount, 0)),
    NOA_DPD_90_plus = sum(ifelse(bucket == "05. 90+ DPD", noa, 0)),
    OS_Total = sum(outstanding_amount),
    NOA_Total = sum(noa),
    NPL= round(((OS_DPD_90_plus/OS_Total)*100),2)) %>% 
  rename(
    Area = area,
    Branch = branch
  ) %>% 
  mutate(across(
    everything(),
    ~ format(.x, 
             big.mark = ".", 
             decimal.mark = ","))
    )

branch_table
```

# DONE
